content.decision_question: Should Hungary adopt a tiered, non-categorical model now on the strength of international precedent, or first build the co-teaching and specialist infrastructure preconditions before attempting structural transfer?
content.evidence_boundary: Comparative implementation data can show what infrastructure was present when transferable models succeeded elsewhere and whether Hungary's current infrastructure gap predicts similar shortfalls. It cannot determine the acceptable sequencing risk of transferring a structural model ahead of infrastructure readiness, which involves a judgment about implementation risk tolerance.
content.tension: TP-live-t3 draws on Wales' and Finland's non-categorical/tiered models as evidence that redesigning categories and gatekeeping can shift measured identification, but Finland's model depends on dual-teacher/SET co-teaching infrastructure that Hungary currently lacks, raising an open question about whether the mechanism is transferable without first building preconditions that TP-live-t5 targets.
content.title: Non-categorical reform transferability: mechanism transfer versus context-dependent infrastructure
value_poles: respecting context-specific infrastructure limits before transfer
